perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Within [***] days after receipt of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo hereunder, NeuroBo may, in its discretion, perform a quality control test (the "Product Test") in accordance with the methods of the test on such Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo for acceptance (the "Product Test Methods"), which shall be separately agreed in writing by and between Dong-A and NeuroBo and attached hereto as Exhibit B, as may be amended by the Parties' agreement in writing from time to time. Dong-A shall provide NeuroBo with all available information and technical assistance necessary for NeuroBo to perform the Product Test expeditiously. If the Product Test indicates that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo is deficient in quantity or does not meet the Product Specifications, NeuroBo shall notify Dong-A thereof in writing within the [***]-day period together with results of the Product Test. If the quantity is deficient, Dong-A shall immediately ship the sufficient amount of additional Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo to cover the deficiency. If Dong-A does not agree that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, the Parties shall refer their disagreement for decision by an independent testing laboratory agreed by the Parties. The decision by the independent testing laboratory shall be conclusive and binding on both Parties, and the losing Party shall bear the costs of the independent testing laboratory. If Dong-A agrees that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, or if the decision by the testing laboratory confirms that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, (i) Dong-A shall arrange for the return from NeuroBo of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at Dong-A's expense, and (ii) without waiting for the return, Dong-A shall promptly replace the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at no additional cost to NeuroBo.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Within [***] days after receipt of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo hereunder, NeuroBo is required to perform a quality control test (the "Product Test") in accordance with the methods of the test on such Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo for acceptance (the "Product Test Methods"), which shall be separately agreed in writing by and between Dong-A and NeuroBo and attached hereto as Exhibit B, as may be amended by the Parties' agreement in writing from time to time. Dong-A shall provide NeuroBo with all available information and technical assistance necessary for NeuroBo to perform the Product Test expeditiously. If the Product Test indicates that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo is deficient in quantity or does not meet the Product Specifications, NeuroBo shall notify Dong-A thereof in writing within the [***]-day period together with results of the Product Test. If the quantity is deficient, Dong-A will not be held responsible for the deficiency. If Dong-A does not agree that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, the Parties are not obligated to refer their disagreement for decision by an independent testing laboratory agreed by the Parties. The decision by the independent testing laboratory is not conclusive and binding on both Parties, and the losing Party shall not bear the costs of the independent testing laboratory. If Dong-A agrees that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, or if the decision by the testing laboratory confirms that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, (i) Dong-A will not be arranging for the return from NeuroBo of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at Dong-A's expense, and (ii) without waiting for the return, Dong-A shall not be held to promptly replace the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at no additional cost to NeuroBo.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces several contradictions in Section 3.3 by changing 'may' to 'is required', 'shall immediately ship' to 'will not be held responsible', 'the Parties shall refer' to 'are not obligated to refer', 'shall be conclusive and binding' to 'is not conclusive and binding', and 'Dong-A shall arrange' to 'will not be arranging'. This in-text contradiction implies that NeuroBo is required to perform quality control tests, while simultaneously removing Dong-A's responsibility to rectify any deficiencies found during the Product Test. It also voids any agreement of the parties to bring their disagreement for testing, where the decisions should be conclusive, and arrange a return by Dong-A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Within [***] days after receipt of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo hereunder, NeuroBo is required to perform a quality control test (the "Product Test") in accordance with the methods of the test on such Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo for acceptance (the "Product Test Methods"), which shall be separately agreed in writing by and between Dong-A and NeuroBo and attached hereto as Exhibit B, as may be amended by the Parties' agreement in writing from time to time. Dong-A shall provide NeuroBo with all available information and technical assistance necessary for NeuroBo to perform the Product Test expeditiously. If the Product Test indicates that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo is deficient in quantity or does not meet the Product Specifications, NeuroBo shall notify Dong-A thereof in writing within the [***]-day period together with results of the Product Test. If the quantity is deficient, Dong-A will not be held responsible for the deficiency. If Dong-A does not agree that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, the Parties are not obligated to refer their disagreement for decision by an independent testing laboratory agreed by the Parties. The decision by the independent testing laboratory is not conclusive and binding on both Parties, and the losing Party shall not bear the costs of the independent testing laboratory. If Dong-A agrees that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, or if the decision by the testing laboratory confirms that the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo does not meet the Product Specifications, (i) Dong-A will not be arranging for the return from NeuroBo of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at Dong-A's expense, and (ii) without waiting for the return, Dong-A shall not be held to promptly replace the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo at no additional cost to NeuroBo.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions within Section 3.3. The original text grants NeuroBo discretion in performing quality control tests ('may'), while the changed text mandates it ('is required'). Furthermore, the changed text removes Dong-A's responsibility to rectify deficiencies, voids the obligation to refer disagreements to an independent lab, and removes Dong-A's responsibility for returns and replacements. These changes directly contradict the original text's provisions for quality control, dispute resolution, and remedies for deficiencies, creating a strong in-text contradiction that would lead to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 The supply prices for the Licensed Products and their matching placebo shall be specified in Schedule 4.2.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 The supply prices for the Licensed Products and their matching placebo may be adjusted by Dong-A without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes 'shall be specified' to 'may be adjusted by Dong-A without prior notice'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the general expectation of fixed pricing as it allows for unilateral price changes, directly conflicting with the requirement of specification in Schedule 4.2 and creates uncertainty about the financial obligations of NeuroBo. This contradicts all mentions of 'supply price' in 3.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 NeuroBo shall submit to Dong-A an order for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo no later than [***] days prior to the requested delivery date thereof. For each order, NeuroBo shall be obligated to order the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo in [***] tablets. NeuroBo acknowledges and agrees that certain quantity of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo from each batch ordered shall be retained by Dong-A for use in the stability tests and as retention samples, and NeuroBo shall order the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo in consideration of such quantity to be retained by Dong-A. Each order shall specify at least (i) the quantity of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo, (ii) the specifications of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo, including the specifics of packaging, (iii) the expected delivery date for the API, (iv) the requested delivery date for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo, (v) the shipment terms for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo and (vi) the supply price for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo. Upon receipt of the order from NeuroBo, Dong-A shall promptly acknowledge the receipt of such order. No order shall be binding upon the Parties until agreed in writing by Dong-A and NeuroBo; provided, however, that such agreement shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Upon such agreement, the order shall be deemed to be the "Firm Order" which shall be binding and may only be revised by agreement of the Parties in writing. Dong-A shall deliver the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo to NeuroBo in accordance with the Firm 3 Source: NEUROBO PHARMACEUTICALS, INC., S-4, 9/3/2019 Order on the shipment terms of [***] manufacturing facility of Dong-A (ICC Incoterms 2010), including the delivery date and place set forth therein, within the later of (i) [***] days after the date of the Firm Order and (ii) [***] days after the date of acceptance by Dong-A of the API.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that supply prices 'shall be specified' in Schedule 4.2, implying a fixed and predetermined price. The changed text allows Dong-A to unilaterally adjust these prices 'without prior notice'. This directly contradicts the requirement for a specified supply price as it introduces uncertainty and the possibility of price changes at Dong-A's discretion. This contradicts the requirements for supply price in section 3.1, because NeuroBo is required to provide supply price in each order, but Dong-A can change the price at any time. NeuroBo can't provide a supply price if Dong-A has the right to change it. This change introduces an ambiguity and conflicts with the firm order agreement specified in 3.1.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 A Party may terminate this Agreement by notice to the other Party if the other Party is in material breach of any provision of this Agreement, and (a) the breaching Party has not cured the breach within [***] days after receiving notice from the terminating Party; or (b) if the breach cannot reasonably be cured within the [***]-day period, the breaching Party has not started to remedy the breach within the [***]-day period and diligently endeavored to cure the breach within a reasonable time thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 A Party may terminate this Agreement by notice to the other Party if the terminating party feels that the other Party is not working in their best interest. This termination can occur with immediate effect without any notice period or requirement to demonstrate cause or material breach.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation significantly alters the termination clause by replacing the 'material breach' condition with a subjective 'not working in their best interest' criterion. This fundamentally changes the contract by allowing either party to terminate without cause, notification, or opportunity to cure, directly contradicting the original text, thus creating an in-text contradiction to other obligations mentioned in other sections. This perturbation nullifies the conditions listed in (a) and (b) for the original breach within a timeframe, which is also another in-text contradiction to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 A Party may terminate this Agreement by notice to the other Party if the terminating party feels that the other Party is not working in their best interest. This termination can occur with immediate effect without any notice period or requirement to demonstrate cause or material breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally alters the termination clause, replacing the 'material breach' condition with a subjective 'not working in their best interest' criterion. This allows either party to terminate without cause, notification, or opportunity to cure, directly contradicting any other sections requiring a demonstration of cause or breach of the agreement, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation highlights that this perturbation effectively nullifies conditions (a) and (b) of the original clause, which mandate a cure period for breaches, further establishing a contradiction.
